description:
Worker runtime logs/state under `.pi/beadwork/workers/runtime` grow large and should be cleaned up as part of worker teardown, especially when the associated worktree is removed.

Scope:
- trace where worker runtime directories/logs are created and retained
- remove runtime artifacts when a worker/worktree is intentionally cleaned up
- avoid deleting state that is still needed for an active worker or for debugging an in-flight failure
- add regression coverage for cleanup behavior and any guardrails

Definition of done:
- tests added/updated under `packages/pi-beadwork-extension/src/__tests__`
- `npm run lint -w @solvedbydev/pi-beadwork-extension`
- `npm run test -w @solvedbydev/pi-beadwork-extension`
- `npm run typecheck -w @solvedbydev/pi-beadwork-extension`

comments.text: Observed in the first real delegated landing after the runtime-cleanup change: landing verified at `70b4db5`, but post-land cleanup still failed before removing the worker worktree/runtime state. Investigation shows the failure is in tmux teardown, not merge-back: `packages/pi-beadwork-extension/src/tmux.ts` still calls `tmux kill-window -t <session>:<window>` and treats a missing target as fatal. In this run the worker window was already gone by cleanup time, so tmux returned `can't find pane`, `cleanupLandedWorker()` aborted, and manual cleanup of the stale worktree/runtime/registry entry was required. This likely affects other delegated tickets using `cleanup-after-landing` (including the active `sbdpi-ik2.5` worker).

title: Clean up `.pi/beadwork/workers/runtime` artifacts when workers/worktrees are removed
